name: International Refugee Assistance Project (IRAP) — Free Legal Services
description: IRAP provides free legal assistance to refugees and displaced people worldwide. Their team of lawyers and caseworkers helps displaced individuals identify and access pathways to safety, including family reunification, humanitarian visas, humanitarian corridors, and resettlement programs. IRAP operates offices in the US, Germany, Jordan, and Lebanon, and coordinates a global network of pro bono attorneys.
eligibility: Refugees, asylum seekers, and displaced persons seeking legal pathways to safety. No income requirements.
applicationProcess: Submit an intake form through the IRAP website or contact a local office for case evaluation.
documentsRequired: Identification documents, proof of displacement or refugee status

name: Equal Rights Beyond Borders — Refugee Family Reunification Legal Aid
description: Equal Rights Beyond Borders provides free legal assistance to refugees and asylum seekers in Europe, specializing in family reunification cases. The organization helps separated refugee families navigate complex European legal processes to reunite, primarily operating in Germany and Greece. They handle cases involving the Dublin III Regulation and provide representation in courts.
eligibility: Refugees and asylum seekers in Europe, particularly those with family reunification cases in Germany and Greece.
applicationProcess: Contact the organization through their website to request legal assistance with family reunification.
documentsRequired: Proof of refugee status, family documentation, identification documents

name: Refugee Legal Support — Free Legal Aid for Asylum Seekers
description: Refugee Legal Support provides free legal information, advice, and representation to asylum seekers and refugees in Europe. Operating projects in France and Greece, they assist individuals with asylum applications, appeals, and family reunification cases. Their team includes qualified lawyers and trained legal volunteers who offer direct legal support at key border points and detention facilities.
eligibility: Asylum seekers and refugees in France and Greece. No income or nationality restrictions.
applicationProcess: Contact through website or visit in-person legal clinics in France and Greece.
documentsRequired: Identification documents if available; services provided regardless of documentation status

name: European Council on Refugees and Exiles (ECRE) — Legal Support Network
organization: European Council on Refugees and Exiles
description: ECRE is a pan-European alliance of 124 NGOs across 40 countries working to protect the rights of refugees, asylum seekers, and displaced persons. Through its legal support programs and the ELENA (European Legal Network on Asylum) network, ECRE coordinates strategic litigation and provides legal resources to help refugees access their rights through European courts and legal systems. The network connects individuals with qualified legal practitioners in their country.
eligibility: Refugees, asylum seekers, and displaced persons across Europe seeking legal support.
applicationProcess: Visit the ECRE website to locate member organizations in your country or access the ELENA legal network.

name: Canada Interim Housing Assistance Program (IHAP)
description: The Interim Housing Assistance Program provides federal funding to support asylum claimants in Canada in achieving housing independence. It funds interim housing and shelter services as well as related supports. Grants are available to provincial, territorial, and municipal governments, while non-profit organizations can receive contribution funding. As of 2026-27, recipients must demonstrate a minimum of 50% matching provincial/territorial/municipal funding.
eligibility: Provincial/territorial/municipal governments and registered non-profit organizations supporting asylum claimants in Canada.
amount: Varies by jurisdiction; requires 50% matching funding
applicationProcess: Apply through IRCC's grants and contributions portal. Organizations must demonstrate matching funding from provincial/territorial/municipal sources.
documentsRequired: Proof of organizational status, project proposal, budget with matching funding commitments

name: Colorado Migrant Support Grant (ARPA Funding)
organization: Colorado Division of Homeland Security and Emergency Management
description: Colorado's Migrant Support Grant provides ARPA (American Rescue Plan Act) funding to organizations helping newly arrived migrants transition from emergency shelters into stable housing and become self-sufficient. The program supports direct services including housing placement, case management, employment readiness, and integration services for migrants in Colorado communities.
eligibility: Non-profit organizations and local governments in Colorado providing migrant support services.
applicationProcess: Apply through the DHSEM website. Applications reviewed on a rolling basis.
documentsRequired: Organizational documentation, project plan, budget, proof of service to migrant populations

name: New York Bureau of Refugee and Immigrant Assistance (BRIA) Programs
organization: New York State Office of Temporary and Disability Assistance
description: The Bureau of Refugee and Immigrant Assistance (BRIA) administers comprehensive programs supporting refugees and immigrants in New York State. Services include resettlement assistance, housing placement, employment training, English language instruction, case management, and connections to healthcare and legal resources. BRIA coordinates with local resettlement agencies to provide wrap-around support for newly arrived refugees and eligible immigrants.
eligibility: Refugees, asylees, Cuban/Haitian entrants, Special Immigrant Visa holders, and certain other immigrant populations in New York State.
amount: Varies by program; includes cash assistance, housing support, and employment services
applicationProcess: Contact a local resettlement agency in New York State or the BRIA office directly.
documentsRequired: Proof of immigration status, identification, proof of New York residency

name: National Immigration Law Center (NILC) — Federal Benefits Eligibility Guide
description: The National Immigration Law Center provides a comprehensive, regularly updated guide to federal programs available to immigrants based on their immigration status. The guide covers eligibility for housing assistance (Section 8, public housing), food programs (SNAP, WIC), healthcare (Medicaid, CHIP), cash assistance (TANF, SSI), and other safety-net programs. This is an essential resource for immigrants and service providers to understand which benefits are accessible based on specific immigration categories.
eligibility: All immigrants in the United States seeking information about federal program eligibility. Resource is freely available online.
applicationProcess: Access the guide freely on the NILC website. No application required.
